rxn_class_summary: Cobalt-catalyzed electrochemical C–H/N–H annulation of 8-aminoquinoline benzamides with alkenes/alkynes to form isoquinolinones
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation regenerates the active high-valent Co(III)/Co(IV) species from Co(II) after C–H metalation, enabling catalytic turnover
electron_flow: Co(II)(acac)2 coordinates the benzamide N–H (deprotonated by NaOPiv) and undergoes CMD-type ortho C–H metalation; alkene insertion and C–N reductive elimination give the isoquinolinone with release of Co(I/II), which is reoxidized at the GF anode to regenerate the active Co(III) catalyst.
rate_determining_step: Not the C–H cleavage step (kH/kD = 1.2 indicates C–H activation is not rate-limiting)

probe_reagents.name: [D5]-benzamide 1a
probe_reagents.observation: Parallel reactions gave 3aa (86%) and [D4]-3aa (80%) with kH/kD = 1.2, indicating C–H cleavage is not rate-determining

probe_reagents.name: D-labeled substrate (H/D exchange)
probe_reagents.observation: H/D exchange experiment performed to probe reversibility of C–H metalation

notes: Mechanism inferred from standard Co(III)/8-aminoquinoline-directed C–H activation paradigm combined with anodic reoxidation; only KIE and H/D exchange probes shown in the provided pages. No radical traps or CV data presented here.
